verdict: All three LinkedIn posts and newsletter meet the depth and specificity standard. The commitment sequence framework is applied with clear VRM grounding, concrete scenarios, and operator-specific scripts throughout. Format compliance is consistent across all pieces.
strengths: Post 1 correctly identifies the structural gap (missing commitment foundation vs. weak pitch) and avoids the common mistake of summarizing the book rather than arguing a point.
Post 2 draws the meeting-vs-change distinction with precision and provides a usable script that is conversational without sounding scripted.
Post 3 names the co-ownership dynamic directly with a specific scenario and a concrete ask that operators can deploy immediately.
Newsletter builds genuinely beyond the posts: the full six-commitment diagnostic is new material that extends the framework rather than repeating the week's posts.
All pieces maintain the operator voice consistently: direct, practical, low-ego, grounded in real acquisition scenarios.
problems: Post 1 is at the upper end of character length and should be checked against the 2,900 character limit during sharpening.
Post 2's script section is clean but the 'dissatisfied owner version' section could be trimmed slightly without losing substance.
Newsletter's NEXT WEEK section is brief but functional; could be made more specific once the Sell Without Selling Out content is drafted.
required_fixes: Verify character count on Post 1 does not exceed 2,900 characters including footer.
No em dashes detected in any piece. Confirm during sharpening pass.
No exclamation points outside direct-speech scripts. Confirm during sharpening pass.
